tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or glomerular pathology. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, raising concern for hypoglycemia. Mild hyperchloremia is present. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and hypoglycemia.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
